summary: Ukrainian forces reported extensive drone activity across multiple fronts, striking Russian locations including St. Petersburg, Krasnodar, and the Mariupol port. Russian forces continued strikes on Ukrainian cities such as Zaporizhzhia, Kharkiv, and Dnipro, while civilian casualties were reported in Bryansk and Lgov. Both sides also noted heightened aerial threats on the eastern and southern fronts.
key_points: Ukrainian drones attacked the port of Mariupol and over 50 UAVs operated over Odessa (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30722).
Russian forces struck Zaporizhzhia, Kryvyi Rih, Dnipro, Kharkiv, and Poltava (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30722).
A drone attack injured a civilian woman in Demyanki, Bryansk Region (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30722).
Ukrainian drones hit an agricultural firm in Kozyrevka, Kursk Region, injuring three people (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30722).
Multiple drone attacks in Belgorod region caused fatalities and injuries in Shebekino, Otradnoe, Yasnye Zori, Urazovo, and Otradovsky (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30722).
Ukrainian forces reported infantry battles and limited advances on the Sumy front, including a 700‑meter gain in sixteen sectors (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30722).
Russian‑linked tactical aviation activity was noted on the south‑east direction, threatening front‑line areas (Source: @kpszsu/64241).
Two Shahed‑136 style strike drones were launched from Kursk and Oryol regions (Source: @eRadarrua/95669).

sections.executive_summary: Ukrainian drone operations have intensified, targeting Russian ports and cities, while Russian air strikes continue against key Ukrainian urban centers, causing civilian casualties and infrastructure damage.

points: Ukrainian drones destroyed more than 86 enemy drones near St. Petersburg and conducted attacks on the Mariupol port, indicating expanded strike range (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30722).

Ukrainian forces reported infantry battles on the Sumy front, with paratroopers advancing up to 700 meters across sixteen sectors and ongoing fights in Ivolzhansk, Pisarevka, and Nova Sich (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30722).

On the Kharkiv front, assault units of the "North" Army Group pushed toward the logistics village of Kachiy Lopan', while fierce firefights occurred in Shevchenko and surrounding villages (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30722).

Ukrainian drones attacked an agricultural firm in Kozyrevka, Kursk Region, injuring three civilians, and also struck a gas station area in Lgov, damaging a fuel truck and wounding a 64‑year‑old woman (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30722).

In the Belgorod region, Ukrainian FPV drones hit a car in Shebekino (driver killed) and caused shrapnel injuries in multiple villages, demonstrating continued cross‑border UAV pressure (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30722).
Russian forces launched strikes on Ukrainian cities Zaporizhzhia, Kryvyi Rih, Dnipro, Kharkiv, and Poltava, reflecting a broad strategic air campaign (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30722).
A drone attack in Bryansk Region injured a civilian woman in the village of Demyanki, showing Russian defensive use of UAVs against Ukrainian incursions (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30722).
Russian tactical aviation activity was reported on the south‑east direction, with threats of aerial weapons deployment for front‑line areas (Source: @kpszsu/64241).
Two Shahed‑136 style strike drones were launched from the Kursk and Oryol regions, indicating Russian use of Iranian‑derived loitering munitions (Source: @eRadarrua/95669).
A Russian‑linked drone strike in Lgov damaged a fuel truck and injured a 64‑year‑old woman, highlighting the use of UAVs against logistical targets (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30722).

Ukrainian infantry battles continued in the Sumy front, with the GRU "North" engaged in Bachevsk and surrounding areas, and a counter‑attack attempt near Ulanovo destroyed (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30722).
Ukrainian paratroopers advanced up to 700 meters in sixteen sectors on the Sumy front, with ongoing fights in Ivolzhansk, Pisarevka, and Nova Sich (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30722).
In the Kharkiv front, the "North" Army Group advanced toward Kachiy Lopan' and faced fierce firefights in Shevchenko, Okhrymovka, and Losevka (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30722).
Ukrainian forces reported attempts to deploy DRGs across the Volchya River via rope crossings, indicating continued river‑crossing operations (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30722).
Russian tactical aviation launched guided bombs over Donetsk region, adding aerial pressure to eastern combat zones (Source: @kpszsu/64191).
A hostile reconnaissance UAV was observed over northern Chernihiv region (Source: @kpszsu/64227).
A reactive UAV was reported heading north‑west over Zaporizhzhia (Source: @kpszsu/64216).
A reactive UAV on the western side of Chernihiv region was seen heading toward Kyiv direction (Source: @kpszsu/64194).
A UAV in Berestyn district, Kharkiv region, was heading toward Poltava, indicating cross‑regional UAV activity (Source: @kpszsu/64191).
Two guided UAVs from the sea were observed heading toward Chornomorske (Source: @eRadarrua/95667).
Ukrainian drones attacked the port of Mariupol, a key southern logistics hub (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30722).
Ukrainian forces reported a reactive UAV heading south‑west toward Zaporizhzhia from the south (Source: @kpszsu/64238).
A reactive UAV was observed north of Poltava, heading south‑west, suggesting pressure on central‑southern routes (Source: @kpszsu/64196).
Ukrainian drone activity over Odessa numbered at least 50 UAVs, indicating significant southern maritime threat (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30722).
Russian attacks on energy infrastructure in Sumy region caused power outages, affecting civilian resilience in the south‑east (Source: @UkraineNow/81732).
Ukrainian forces reported destroying more than 86 enemy drones near St. Petersburg, showing extended air‑defense reach (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30722).
Russian tactical aviation launched guided bombs over Donetsk, increasing aerial pressure on Ukrainian positions (Source: @kpszsu/64191).
Two Shahed‑136 style strike drones were launched from Kursk and Oryol, reflecting Russian use of loitering munitions (Source: @eRadarrua/95669).
Ukrainian drones were shot down on the outskirts of Kaluga and Obninsk, as well as near Moscow approaches (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30722).
A drone attack in Bryansk region injured a civilian woman, indicating Russian defensive UAV deployment (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30722).

key_developments: Ukrainian drones destroyed over 86 enemy drones near St. Petersburg, extending Ukraine's air‑defense reach (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30722).
Russian strikes hit Zaporizhzhia, Kryvyi Rih, Dnipro, Kharkiv, and Poltava, demonstrating a broad offensive campaign (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30722).
Ukrainian forces advanced up to 700 meters across sixteen sectors on the Sumy front, indicating localized gains (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30722).
Two Shahed‑136 style strike drones were launched from Kursk and Oryol regions, showing Russian use of Iranian‑derived loitering munitions (Source: @eRadarrua/95669).
Multiple civilian casualties reported from drone attacks in Bryansk, Lgov, and Belgorod regions, highlighting the conflict's impact on non‑combatants (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30722).
Ukrainian UAVs conducted a major attack on the Mariupol port and over 50 UAVs operated over Odessa, threatening southern logistics (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30722).
Russian tactical aviation activity was noted on the south‑east direction, with threats of aerial weapons for front‑line areas (Source: @kpszsu/64241).
sections.threat_assessment: Both sides are escalating UAV employment, with Ukraine extending strikes deep into Russian territory and Russia intensifying loitering‑munitions launches from western regions. Civilian areas remain vulnerable, as evidenced by multiple drone‑related injuries and fatalities across Bryansk, Kursk, and Belgorod. The proliferation of guided bombs and tactical aviation on the eastern front adds a layer of high‑precision threat to Ukrainian defensive positions.
sections.regional_response: Ukrainian command appears to be reinforcing air‑defense assets near key cities while continuing offensive UAV operations against Russian logistics hubs. Russian authorities are deploying additional tactical aviation and loitering munitions to counter Ukrainian advances, particularly on the southern and eastern fronts. Local administrations in affected Russian regions are reporting civilian casualties, prompting heightened alert levels.
sections.intelligence_notes: Monitoring of UAV launch sites in Kursk, Oryol, and the Black Sea coast is critical for anticipating future strike patterns. The reported 700‑meter Ukrainian advance on the Sumy front suggests possible preparation for deeper penetration. Continued civilian impact in Russian border regions may influence domestic narratives and resource allocation for air‑defense upgrades.
